history.text: Bolivia declared independence or statehood, marking a decisive political break.
The current constitution came into force, setting the basic framework for today's Bolivia.

worthSeeing.title: Salar de Uyuni:
worthSeeing.text: The world's largest salt flat, a surreal mirror landscape at 3,650 m altitude.

worthSeeing.title: Lake Titicaca:
worthSeeing.text: The highest navigable lake on earth, shared with Peru, dotted with reed islands.

worthSeeing.title: Sucre:
worthSeeing.text: Bolivia's constitutional capital, a UNESCO-listed colonial white city.
